---
title: "Hello, welcome to my website!"
---

<br>

```{r out.width = "30%", fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("ERoberts.jpg")
```

<br>

Hi! I am Emily Roberts.  I am a new [assistant professor of biostatistics at the University of Iowa](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/people/emily-roberts/). I completed my PhD at the [Department of Biostatistics, University of Michigan](https://sph.umich.edu/biostat/) upon completion of my dissertation “Causal Inference Methods and Intermediate Endpoints in Randomized Clinical Trials”. I am interested in statistical methodology that can be applied in a cancer setting. Welcome to my page where I have included a bit about myself and my work.

I graduated Summa Cum Laude in 2016 from Coe College with a BA in mathematics and psychology as a member of Phi Beta Kappa National Honor Society. Additionally, I earned my MS in biostatistics from Michigan in 2018. My passion for medical research and ultimate career goals stem from my diagnosis of type I diabetes at the age of four. 


Other links and news

[Rackham Predoctoral Fellowship Recipients](https://rackham.umich.edu/predoctoral-fellowship-celebration/)

[Kohawk Stories](https://www.coe.edu/why-coe/kohawk-stories/emily-roberts)

[McElroy Fellowship](https://www.mcelroytrust.org/success-stories)

[The Gazette](https://www.thegazette.com/higher-education/coe-grad-receives-prestigious-fellowships/)
